context: Tests whether the agent correctly performs pre-flight verification before running multi-model tile evals, including finding the tile safely, verifying scenarios and login, confirming the model configuration, and communicating time expectations.

checklist.name: Excludes .tessl cache
checklist.description: The tile search command or script explicitly excludes paths containing .tessl/ (e.g., uses -not -path '*/.tessl/*' or equivalent)

checklist.name: .tessl/tiles warning
checklist.description: The output warns the user if their tile path is inside a .tessl/tiles/ directory and explains this is the local install cache (not usable for evals)

checklist.name: Scenario existence check
checklist.description: The verification checks for the presence of eval scenario files under the tile's evals/ directory (e.g., checks for evals/*/task.md or equivalent)

checklist.name: Scenario generation guidance
checklist.description: If no scenarios are found, the output provides the tessl scenario generate command (not just a generic message) with the tile path argument

checklist.name: Login verification
checklist.description: The output includes a step to run tessl whoami to verify login status before proceeding

checklist.name: No --workspace flag
checklist.description: The output does NOT mention or include a --workspace flag in any tessl eval context

checklist.name: Default model names
checklist.description: The output lists all three default model identifiers: claude-haiku-4-5, claude-sonnet-4-6, and claude-opus-4-6

checklist.name: Model subset confirmation
checklist.description: The output asks the user to confirm whether to use all three models or a subset (not just assumes all three)

checklist.name: Time estimate provided
checklist.description: The output includes a time estimate per scenario per model (10-15 minutes) or a total estimate formula (e.g., N scenarios × 30-45 minutes)

checklist.name: Run count option
checklist.description: The output asks whether to run each scenario once or multiple times, and mentions that 3 runs is recommended before publishing
